analysis.reasoning: Core message: The U.S. Embassy mourns Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg, praising her as an inspiration tied to the Constitution and rule of law. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Supported, as it highlights reverence for the Constitution and rule of law as enduring American values and legacy, embodied by a Supreme Court Justice. US2 (admirable society) – Partially relevant; it praises an iconic American figure who inspired women, girls, and broader society, but the focus is more on institutional values than on societal diversity or civil society; still, honoring a prominent public servant as a societal inspiration can fit. US3 (hostile enemies) – Not present. US4 (force for good) – Not present; no claim about US actions abroad or defense of oppressed peoples. US5 (great international ally) – Not present. US6 (at the forefront) – Not present. Conclusion: Primarily US1; US2 is arguable but less central.
